parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces critical runtime modules (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, glm-client.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, state.mjs) and a sweeping companion rewrite (glm-companion.mjs) without visible evidence of race-condition hardening, idempotency guarantees, or safe rollback for persisted job state. Multiple new code paths persist state to disk and dispatch to external APIs, yet the diff stat shows process.mjs was *deleted*—a core module replaced mid-stack—raising migration-risk questions. The test suite is substantial but the focus areas (retry, error dispatch, background jobs) are notoriously hard to unit-test for the exact failure modes (concurrent state corruption, partial writes, stale-lock recovery) that would be expensive in production. Ship-blocking concerns follow.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted without visible migration path—imports may silently resolve to undefined at runtime
findings.body: The diff stat shows `scripts/lib/process.mjs` was removed (67 lines deleted). Any downstream module that imported from it via bare specifier or cached require would receive undefined exports at runtime after upgrade, with no migration shim or re-export stub visible in the diff. If users have custom hooks or extensions referencing process.mjs, this is a silent breaking change that won't surface until the code path is exercised.
findings.recommendation: Provide a shim/re-export file at the old path that re-exports from the replacement module(s), or document the breaking change prominently in CHANGELOG.md with a migration guide. Verify no other files in the repo still import from 'scripts/lib/process.mjs'.

findings.title: retry.mjs introduced without visibility into interrupt/reentrancy safety—partial retry state may corrupt persisted jobs
findings.body: A new 203-line retry.mjs module is the backbone of the BigModel auto-retry/backoff feature. Retry logic that spans async boundaries and persists state to tracked-jobs is vulnerable to: (1) process interruption mid-retry leaving stale 'in-progress' state, (2) re-entrancy if two invocations race on the same job ID, (3) exponential backoff clock skew on resume. The diff adds retry.test.mjs (352 lines) but the test file cannot be inspected for coverage of interrupt/reentrancy paths from the summary alone. Given tracked-jobs.mjs writes to disk, a crash during retry could leave a job permanently stuck.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit stale-lock detection and recovery to tracked-jobs.mjs (e.g., TTL-based expiry on 'running' state). Verify retry.test.mjs covers: SIGINT/SIGTERM during retry, concurrent retry on same job ID, and resumption after crash. Add a 'stuck job cleanup' command or automatic recovery on next invocation.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs rewritten (+155/-lines) with new error-dispatch path—unhandled BigModel error subclasses may silently drop into catch-all
findings.body: The companion's API client (glm-client.mjs) was substantially rewritten alongside a new bigmodel-errors.mjs module (168 lines) that classifies errors for retry/retire decisions. If the error classifier does not cover all HTTP status codes or error shapes returned by the BigModel API, unclassified errors will fall through to a default path that may either (a) retry errors that should not be retried (e.g., 401/403 auth failures), or (b) silently swallow errors that should surface to the user. The 189-line test file exists but the classification completeness cannot be verified from the summary.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the error classifier has an explicit 'unclassifiable' category that always surfaces to the user (never retries silently). Add a test that asserts every HTTP status code 400-599 is mapped to a deliberate retry/no-retry decision. Log unclassifiable errors at WARN level for observability.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs state persistence lacks atomic-write guarantee—partial write on crash corrupts job state file
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manages persisted job state, likely writing JSON to disk. If the write is not atomic (write-to-temp + rename), a crash or power loss during write can produce a truncated/corrupt state file that causes all subsequent invocations to fail or lose job history. The fs.mjs utility (44 lines changed) may or may not provide atomic-write primitives.
findings.recommendation: Verify fs.mjs write helpers use write-to-temp-file + atomic rename pattern. Add a test that corrupts the state file and asserts the system recovers gracefully (ignores corrupt file, recreates, or logs a clear error).

findings.title: Background execution mode (--wait/--background flags) may detach processes without reliable lifecycle tracking
findings.body: The review command now supports --wait/--background execution-mode flags (added in v0.4.5 per commit d573807). Background process management in Node.js without a proper process supervisor is fragile: (1) the parent may exit before the child writes its PID file, (2) SIGTERM may not propagate to the detached child, (3) there is no visible heartbeat/watchdog mechanism. If the background job crashes, the user has no way to know without manually checking state.
findings.recommendation: Document the background mode's limitations clearly. Ensure job-control.mjs writes state before detaching. Add a status-check command that reports whether a background job is still alive. Consider adding a timeout-based watchdog that marks jobs as failed if they exceed expected duration.

findings.title: configure-self-hosted forge-protection.sh executes external API calls with potential credential exposure in CI logs
findings.body: A new 126-line shell script configures self-hosted forge branch protection via API calls. If the script logs curl commands with tokens, or if error handling exposes response bodies containing session tokens, credentials could leak into CI logs. The script is in .github/ excluded from path-leak guard per the commit message, but this exclusion itself is concerning.
findings.recommendation: Audit the script for: (1) curl commands that log headers/tokens, (2) error paths that echo full response bodies, (3) ensure the path-leak guard exclusion is intentional and scoped. Add `set +x` before any curl calls to prevent verbose logging of tokens.

next_steps: Inspect the full diff of scripts/lib/process.mjs to confirm all importers have been updated or a re-export shim exists
Read retry.mjs and retry.test.mjs to verify interrupt-safety and stale-lock recovery
Read bigmodel-errors.mjs to confirm exhaustive HTTP status code coverage and safe fallback for unclassifiable errors
Verify tracked-jobs.mjs uses atomic writes via fs.mjs
Test background mode (--background flag) with SIGINT to the parent and verify child lifecycle
Audit configure-self-hosted forge-protection.sh for credential leakage in CI logs
